subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Help the Story Builder crew map each story so every retell has the right parts in order.
lesson.objective: I can identify characters, setting, problem, solution, and sequence of events.
lesson.concept_explanation: Stories have characters in a setting. Many stories include a problem and solution, and events happen in an order that helps readers retell the story.
lesson.common_misconception: Readers may mix up where a story happens with who is in the story, or tell events out of order.
lesson.real_world_connection: Story maps help readers talk about books, movies, and events from their own day.
lesson.lesson_summary: Name the characters and setting, find the problem and solution, then retell the beginning, middle, and end.
lesson.mini_lesson: A good retell includes the most important story elements in the order they happened.

worked_examples.text: Watch: A story map keeps characters, setting, problem, and solution in separate boxes.
worked_examples.correct_answer: They used a map and put the sign back up.
worked_examples.passage: Max wanted to find the nature trail, but the sign had fallen down. He looked at a map with Ana, and they followed the blue path. At the end, they reached the pond and put the sign back up.

story_elements.solution: They used a map and put the sign back up.

worked_examples.text: Demo: Retell the events in the same order they happened.
worked_examples.correct_answer: Max wanted to find the nature trail. -> The sign had fallen down. -> Max and Ana followed the blue path. -> They reached the pond and fixed the sign.

The sign had fallen down.
